name: Pinetop Smith
abstract: Clarence "Pinetop" Smith (June 11, 1904 – March 15, 1929) was an American boogie-woogie style blues pianist. His hit tune "Pinetop's Boogie Woogie" featured rhythmic "breaks" that were an essential ingredient of ragtime music, but also a fundamental foreshadowing of rock and roll.

about_quotes.speaker: Ann Rabson
about_quotes.evidence: my dad got me these albums — Pinetop Smith and Albert Ammons, and Meade Lux Lewis and on and on. So that really influenced me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ann Rabson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 21, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Walter "Furry" Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: The fellow that originated it was called Pine Top Smith. Yeah. Out of Chicago, out here. Did you know him? Pine Top Smith. Or did you just hear his records? I didn't know. I didn't know his records.

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: No. The first time I heard it called Boogie-Woogie was when Pinetop Smith recorded for Mayo {Williams, 1929}* out of Chicago.

about_quotes.speaker: Manuel "Fess" Manetta
about_quotes.evidence: Pine Top Smith. Featured by Tommy Dawson. I have to play Boogie was out. He played it good too.
